marks_distribution: Class XI Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 1 (Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry): 7 Marks
Unit 3 (Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties): 6 Marks
Unit 4 (Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure): 7 Marks
Unit 8 (Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques): 11 Marks
Class XII Theory: Total Marks - 70, Time - 3 Hours
Unit 8 (Aldehydes, Ketones and Carboxylic Acids): 8 Marks
question_paper_design: No chapter wise weightage is provided, however, care to be taken to cover all the chapters.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all the sections.

bloom_levels.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas by organizing, comparing, translating, interpreting, giving descriptions and stating main ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.

Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences and find evidence to support generalizations. Present and defend opinions by making judgments about information, validity of ideas or quality of work based on a set of criteria. Compile information together in a different way by combining elements in a new pattern or proposing alternative solutions.

internal_assessment.scheme_name: Practical Examination for Visually Challenged Learners (Classes XI and XII)
components: Written test (based on given/prescribed practical's): 10 Marks
requirements: Written examination conducted at the time of practical examination.
Question paper contains total of 15 skill-based very short answer questions; student answers any 10.
Student required to maintain a practical file recording at least five listed experiments.
Practicals checked and signed by internal examiner.

experiments: Cutting glass tube and glass rod
Bending a glass tube
Drawing out a glass jet
Boring a cork

experiment_sections.section_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
experiments: Determination of melting point of an organic compound.
Determination of boiling point of an organic compound.
Crystallization of impure sample (Alum, Copper Sulphate, Benzoic Acid).
Determination of pH of solutions from fruit juices/known concentrations.
Comparing the pH of strong and weak acids.
Study pH change in titration using universal indicator.
Study pH change by common-ion effect.
Shift in equilibrium between ferric ions and thiocyanate ions.
Shift in equilibrium between [Co(H2O)6]2+ and chloride ions.
Using mechanical/electronic balance.
Preparation of standard solution of Oxalic acid.
Determination of strength of NaOH by titration against Oxalic acid.
Preparation of standard solution of Sodium carbonate.
Determination of strength of HCl by titration against Sodium Carbonate.
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (specific list provided).
Detection of Nitrogen, Sulphur, Chlorine in organic compounds.
Crystallization (Copper sulphate or Benzoic acid)
Determination of pH using pH paper.
Comparing pH of strong and weak acids.
Shift in equilibrium (Ferric ions/thiocyanate).
Shift in equilibrium ([Co(H2O)6]2+/chloride).
Preparation of standard solution of oxalic acid.
Determination of molarity of NaOH.
Determination of one anion and one cation (specific list provided).
Detection of Nitrogen.
Detection of Halogen.

practical_assessment.assessment_notes: Micro-chemical methods should be used wherever possible. Topics like s & p Block Elements and Gaseous State are assessed formatively only.

experiments: Preparation of lyophilic sol (starch, egg albumin, gum).
Preparation of lyophobic sol (aluminum hydroxide, ferric hydroxide, arsenous sulphide).
Dialysis of sols.
Study role of emulsifying agents.
Effect of concentration and temperature on rate (Sodium Thiosulphate + HCl).
Reaction rates: Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide.
Clock reaction: Potassium Iodate, Sodium Sulphate.
Enthalpy of dissolution (Copper Sulphate or KNO3).
Enthalpy of neutralization (HCl + NaOH).
Determination of enthalpy change (Acetone and Chloroform).
Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with concentration changes.
Separation of pigments (leaves/flowers) and Rf determination.
Separation of constituents in inorganic mixture.
Double salt: Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum.
Potassium Ferric Oxalate.
Acetanilide
Di-benzal acetone
p-Nitroacetanilide
Aniline yellow/2-Naphthol Aniline dye
Unsaturation, alcoholic, phenolic, aldehydic, ketonic, carboxylic, amino groups.
Tests for carbohydrates, fats and proteins in pure samples/foodstuffs.
Determination of concentration/molarity against Oxalic acid.
Determination of concentration/molarity against Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate.
Determination of one anion and one cation in a given salt (specific list provided).
Preparation of lyophilic sol.
Preparation of lyophobic sol.
Separation of pigments and Rf determination.
Alcoholic/Carboxylic groups.
Aldehyde/Ketonic groups.
Tests in given foodstuffs.
Potash Alum
(a) Preparation of standard Oxalic acid solution.
(b) Determination of molarity of KMnO4 against Oxalic acid.
Determination of one anion and one cation (specific list provided).

practical_assessment.assessment_notes: Micro-chemical methods should be used wherever possible. Topics like Surface Chemistry, Isolation of Elements, Polymers, and Everyday Life are assessed formatively only.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_projects: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion.
Study of methods of purification of water.
Testing hardness/presence of Iron/Fluoride/Chloride in drinking water.
Investigation of foaming capacity of washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate.
Study acidity of different samples of tea leaves.
Determination of rate of evaporation of different liquids.
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers.
Study acidity of fruit and vegetable juices.

project_assessment.description: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources. Any investigatory project involving about 10 periods can be chosen with teacher approval.
suggested_projects: Study of presence of oxalate ions in guava fruit at different stages of ripening.
Study of quantity of casein present in milk samples.
Preparation of soybean milk and comparison with natural milk (curd formation, temperature effect).
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative under various conditions.
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase; effect of pH and temperature.
Comparative study of fermentation rate: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice.
Extraction of essential oils (Saunf, Ajwain, Illaichi).
Study of common food adulterants in fat, oil, butter, sugar, turmeric powder, chili powder, pepper.
